summary: Investigator correctly aborted without a fix when the local sandbox couldn't run JAX/TPU code, and produced an honest, well-cited static audit of five plausible latent issues.

payload.summary: Investigator correctly aborted without a fix when the local sandbox couldn't run JAX/TPU code, and produced an honest, well-cited static audit of five plausible latent issues.

checks.label: hypothesis_coverage
checks.note: Five hypotheses span loss-side (mask_token_id default), likelihood integration (SNR grid), schedule wiring (hybrid_scale), data pipeline (dedup), and shuffle finalization — a reasonable cross-section of reproducibility failure classes for a scaling-law repo.

checks.label: evidence_quality
checks.note: Every static claim is tied to a specific file:line (e.g., loss.py:27/85, likelihood.py:155/167–175, diffusion_trainer.py:76, data.py:162) and the environment refutation in f1 is concrete about missing jax/flax and the absence of a lightweight eval entrypoint.

checks.label: fix_minimality
checks.note: No fix was applied; the `## Fix applied:` section correctly states none was attempted because no executable metric delta was producible — consistent with the operating contract.

checks.label: causal_link
checks.note: No metric delta was reported and none was claimed, so there is no spurious causal link to audit; the investigator explicitly declined to assert causation without runtime evidence.

checks.label: alternative_explanations
checks.note: The dossier notes the published PPLs may come from the `lm-evaluation-harness@gidd-v2` fork rather than `likelihood.py`, but does not enumerate other non-bug explanations (e.g., checkpoint selection, tokenizer mismatch, SNR flag defaults) with equal weight when discussing the h2 uncertainty.

checks.label: uncertainty_honesty
checks.note: `Remaining uncertainty` is substantive: it names five specific unverified items (external GiddLoss callers, ELBO grid bias, harness provenance, upstream dedup, TPU-gated discrimination) rather than generic hedging.

checks.label: suggested_followup
checks.note: Clone `dvruette/lm-evaluation-harness@gidd-v2` and diff its diffusion-model likelihood integrator against `gidd_easydel/likelihood.py` — this is the cheapest static check that would discriminate h2/h3 without needing TPUs or the 1.3 TB dataset, and the investigator flagged it as unknown but did not attempt it.
